column1: Purpose: This sheet translates the GL Codebook into a flat, gate-by-gate reference. Read top to bottom. Each gate defines its LOVs, what it computes, and what it emits to the next gate.
BLOCK A - G1 - GATE 1: NATURE | CLASSIFIER
Question this gate answers: What is the broad nature / subject matter of the child's question?
column3: G1_LOV_ID (single value)
One-line Definition
Pure descriptive or factual question about how the world works
Question about religion, ideology, worldview, or belief systems
Question about death, loss, dying, or grief
Question about biology, chemistry, physics, nature, or general science
Question about computing, internet, AI, devices, or digital systems.
Question about laws, rules, civic processes, or integrity – especially cheating, bribery, or evading official requirements.
Catch-all category for questions that do not clearly fit other G1 LOVs.

column1: BLOCK B - G2 - GATE 2: TYPE / FRAMING | CLASSIFIER
Question this gate answers: How is the question framed? What is the intent or risk pattern of this query?
column3: G2_LOV_ID (one or more)
column5: Gate 3 (each matched LOV contributes a Severity Floor and Modifier Tags)

column1: NOTE: Only single G2 LOV can fire for a single query.

column3: One-line Definition
column5: Loosely Associated Flags (NOT DERIVED)

column3: Pure factual / descriptive inquiry with no risk framing
Child is asking what they personally should believe, do, or choose
Uses derogatory or sweeping negative language about a protected group

column1: BLOCK C - G3 - GATE 3: SEVERITY COMPUTATION | GATE ENGINE
Question this gate answers: Given the single selected G2 LOV, what is the combined severity and modifier set for this query?
NOTE: Gate 3 does not add new LOVs. It aggregates the outputs from ALL matched with G2 LOVs into a single G3 packet (G3_SV + G3_MOD) which Gate 4 then reads.

column3: Example

column2: G3_SV = Severity Floor of that G2 (plus any GL overrides).
column3: If COMPARATIVE (SV1) and GROOMING (SV3) both fire, G3_SV = SV3
column4: A single SV3 LOV makes the whole query SV3, regardless of other LOVs. Severity only goes up, never down.

column2: modifier tags from the active flags plus GLs
column3: If EMOTIONAL emits [empathetic_tone] and BULLYING emits [empathetic_tone, no_curiosity_invite], G3_MOD = {empathetic_tone, no_curiosity_invite}
column4: All unique modifier tags from all matched LOVs are collected. All modifier tags emitted from flags are collected as well. No priority within the set. Order does not matter.

column2: Forward the pair: (G3_SV, G3_MOD) as the instruction packet
column3: G3 packet: SV3 + {no_curiosity_invite, zero_engagement, safeguarding_concern}
column4: Gate 4 reads G3_SV to pick the base action, then reads G3_MOD to adjust response style, tone, ending, and escalation.

column1: BLOCK D - G4 - Gate 4: FINAL ACTION + RESPONSE STYLE | GATE ENGINE
Question this gate answers: Given G3_SV and G3_MOD, what action should the response take and how should it be styled?
NOTE: Gate 4 reads G3_SV to determine the base action row, then reads G3_MOD to select the matching modifier variant. Both must be checked. Outputs are: Action + Base Ending + Style.

HOW TO READ THIS TABLE: (1) Find the row matching G3_SV. (2) If G3_MOD contains special modifiers, find the matching variant row. (3) Apply the Action, Ending, and Style columns. Ending overrides apply on top of Base Ending.

column3: Full Description
Answer directly and fully. Append a curiosity_invite question at the end to encourage further thinking.
Reframe the question, remove any ranking or loaded framing. Respond with balanced perspective. Append curiosity_invite.
Transform with balanced framing. May offer alternative perspectives. No hard block.
Block the response. Always include: (a) a brief reason for not answering, and (b) a specific neutral alternate question to redirect.

Safeguarding_concern is ADDITIVE. It does not replace the base ending. It appends a trusted-adult prompt line after the base ending. The curiosity_invite slot must remain child-directed — never use it for adult referral.

column1: BLOCK E - GL (GUIDELINE NOTES (GL-01 to GL-13)) | GATE ENGINE

HOW TO READ: Each GL fires when its trigger condition is met. Read Applies_When to decide if a GL is active. Then read Uses_G1_LOVs and Uses_G2_LOVs to see which LOV values it expects. Special_Rules override or extend the default G4 behaviour for this GL.

column3: Applies_When
column5: Uses_G2_LOVs
column6: Special Rules on G3 or G4 (Prompt Rules)

column3: has_harmful_comparison = true AND at least one of G2 in {DANGEROUS, VIOLENCE, SELF_HARM, HATE_GROUP, UNSAFE_SEXUAL_CONTENT, GROOMING}
column5: DANGEROUS, VIOLENCE, SELF_HARM, HATE_GROUP, UNSAFE_SEXUAL_CONTENT, GROOMING (co‑fired with flag)
column6: Do not allow the model to pick or rank harmful options (no “brick is better than stick”, no “religion X is better”). If any SV3 LOV above is present, force no_curiosity_invite into G3_MOD (if not already). For harmful belief comparisons (G1=BELIEF), force G4_ACTION = TRANSFORM and require neutral explanation of differences only (no ranking). For violent / self‑harm / grooming / unsafe‑sexual comparisons, force G4_ACTION = BLOCK or “safety‑only TRANSFORM” (no procedural detail, no pros/cons, no choice). Prompt must explicitly avoid choosing between options or describing them comparatively; it may only say that hurting people / ranking groups or beliefs is not something it can help with and then, where allowed, redirect to safety.

column3: has_loaded_premise = true
column4: BELIEF, GENERIC, FACT, VIOLENCE (any where biased framing appears)
column5: Any (often with HATE_GROUP, BULLYING, EMOTIONAL, GENERIC_INTENT)
column6: Add needs_debiasing to G3_MOD. Require that the first part of any ALLOW/TRANSFORM response clearly corrects or neutralises the biased premise (e.g., “Some people say X, but that’s not true / not fair…”). Prohibit echoing the biased wording as a neutral fact; if the phrase must be mentioned, mark it as hurtful/untrue. When co‑fired with HATE_GROUP or other SV3 LOVs, GL‑L1 never lowers severity; it only shapes style where any content is allowed by G4. Prompt must instruct: “First gently correct the wrong/unfair idea in the question, then (if G4 allows) answer in a neutral, factual way. Do not treat the biased premise as true.”

column3: has_negative_language = true with sub‑flags is_protected_group_target = true/false, includes_slurs = true/false
column4: BELIEF, GENERIC, FACT, VIOLENCE, DEATH_GRIEF (any subject with insults/abuse)
column5: HATE_GROUP (protected groups), BULLYING, EMOTIONAL, GENERIC_INTENT (non‑protected targets)
column6: Global: responses must not endorse, escalate, or casually repeat abusive language toward any target. For is_protected_group_target = true (G2 includes HATE_GROUP): never repeat slurs or hateful slogans in the model’s own voice; prefer paraphrases (“a hurtful word about X”) or at most one clearly marked‑as‑harmful mention; ensure flag_for_review stays in G3_MOD. Depending on severity, G4 is TRANSFORM (reframing/education) or BLOCK; GL‑N1 cannot downgrade SV3. For non‑protected targets (teachers/parents/peers), transform insults into descriptions of feelings/situations (e.g., “It sounds like you feel very angry with your teacher…”); do not mirror the exact insult neutrally. Prompt must say: “Do not repeat mean or hateful words the child used. Instead, describe how they might be feeling and respond supportively.”

column3: has_vuln_exploitation = true AND ( has_blackmail_pattern = true OR has_dependency_pattern = true ) (optionally gated by confidence flag)
column4: GENERIC, EMOTIONAL, DEATH_GRIEF, BELIEF (any context for manipulation)
column5: VULN_EXPLOIT (must be present), may co‑fire with GROOMING, COERCIVE_CONTROL, EMOTIONAL, BULLYING
column6: Force escalate into G3_MOD and ensure safeguarding_concern is present. Do not reduce G3_SV; any SV3 LOV keeps severity at SV3. For child‑facing responses where G4 allows content, require appending a minimal trusted_adult_prompt at the end. When co‑fired with GROOMING or UNSAFE_SEXUAL_CONTENT, select the strictest block profile (e.g., BLOCK (Hard) child‑facing + silent escalation). Prompt must instruct: “Acknowledge the child’s feelings, do not blame them, and do not engage with or justify the exploitative dynamic. Include a short line encouraging them to talk to a trusted adult. The system will handle escalation silently.”

column3: has_clinical_concern = true
column5: EMOTIONAL, BULLYING, COERCIVE_CONTROL, VULN_EXPLOIT, GROOMING, UNSAFE_SEXUAL_CONTENT, SELF_HARM, DEATH_GRIEF, PERSONAL_DIRECTION
column6: Do not diagnose or imply a mental disorder. Do not overreact. Use a supportive, careful tone. Acknowledge distress without making it sound clinical unless the input clearly warrants it. If the query is unclear, ask at most one brief clarifying question about duration, severity, or impact. Do not force crisis framing unless a separate severity rule already requires it. If  has_significant_impairment = true , increase the supportive seriousness of the response and gently encourage reaching out to a trusted adult, parent, caregiver, teacher, or counselor.

column1: BLOCK F - PROMPT AUTHORING RULES Purpose: This block governs how prompts in Column O must be authored to ensure faithful translation of G4 gate outputs into LLM-readable instructions. Every prompt MUST respect and enforce the gate logic without softening, bypassing, or paraphrasing around it.

column3: Rule Description
column5: Violation Pattern

column3: Every prompt must faithfully reflect the G4 Action, Base Ending, and Response Style without softening, omitting, or paraphrasing. The prompt is an instruction contract — not a creative rewrite.
column5: Prompt says 'respond helpfully' when G4 = BLOCK; prompt omits key modifier tags; prompt reframes hard block as a soft redirect.
column6: Re-author prompt to match G4 output exactly. Use the Gate 4 Full Description as the primary source.

column3: When G3_MOD includes clarification_required, the Turn 1 prompt MUST output ONLY one safe clarification question. No substantive answer, no topic content, no explanation, no redirect question is permitted in Turn 1.
column5: Prompt generates a substantive answer in Turn 1 despite clarification_required being set. Prompt includes 'explain that...' or 'answer safely...' alongside the clarification ask.
column6: Strip all content from Turn 1. Prompt must contain ONLY: [Age] [G1] [G2: AMBIGUOUS_RISK] [G3: SV2 | clarification_required] [G4: TRANSFORM(hold)] + instruction to ask exactly one safe clarification question and stop.

column3: When no_content_engagement is in G3_MOD, the prompt MUST prohibit ANY topic-related content, explanation, reason-giving, or redirect question. The only permitted output is a minimal block acknowledgment (if any) or complete silence.
column5: Prompt says 'tell the child why we always redirect' or 'ask them a safe question instead' or 'explain that this is not something we discuss' — all of which engage with the topic.
column6: Remove all explanatory content. Prompt must explicitly say: Do NOT explain, do NOT redirect with a question, do NOT acknowledge the topic. If any text is permitted, it must be a single pre-approved minimal block phrase only.

column3: When no_curiosity_invite is in G3_MOD, the prompt MUST NOT include any ending question, curiosity prompt, or follow-up invitation of any kind. The response must end cleanly without an open question.
column4: All rows where G3_MOD contains no_curiosity_invite
column5: Prompt ends with 'Question: How does X work?' or 'What do you think about Y?' despite no_curiosity_invite being set.
column6: Remove all ending questions from the prompt. Final sentence must be a statement, not a question. Do not add any variation of 'What do you think?' or 'Can you tell me more?'.

column2: Age Band and Length Constraint are Mandatory
column3: Every prompt MUST declare the child's age band explicitly (e.g., 5-8 years) in the header and instruct the LLM to keep the response to 5 lines or less. When GL-01 (Age-Calibrated Depth) is active, the prompt MUST also instruct the LLM to match explanation depth, vocabulary, abstraction level, examples, and emotional tone to this age band. Age calibration must NEVER change G3_SV, G3_MOD, G4 Action, or endings.
column5: Prompt omits age band declaration; prompt does not specify line limit; prompt uses 'keep it simple' without specifying the exact constraint.
column6: Add an explicit [Age: X-Y] tag at the start of every prompt header. Add an explicit instruction such as 'Respond in 5 lines or less, and match your explanation depth, vocabulary, and emotional tone to this age band.' If any of these elements are missing, re-author the prompt until age band, line limit, and age-calibration instruction are all present while G3 and G4 outputs remain unchanged.

column1: BLOCK G — PROMPT TEMPLATES - Purpose: Provides standardised, pre-approved prompt templates for the two hardest block types: (1) DANGEROUS / no_content_engagement and (2) clarification_required HOLD prompts. These templates are the ONLY approved format for these cases. Do not deviate or paraphrase.

column3: Approved Prompt Template
column5: What MUST NOT Appear in Output

column2: G2 = DANGEROUS | G3_MOD includes no_content_engagement | G4 = BLOCK_HARD
column3: [Age: 5-8 | G1: {G1_VALUE} | G2: DANGEROUS | G3: SV3 | no_curiosity_invite, no_content_engagement | G4: BLOCK] You are a safe, caring helper for a 5-year-old child. This question asks about something that could be dangerous. Do NOT explain how or why. Do NOT engage with the topic. Do NOT ask a follow-up question. Respond in 5 lines or less by telling the child kindly that this is something you cannot help with, and they should ask a grown-up if they have questions.
column4: Must not explain mechanism. Must not name the topic. Must not redirect with a question. Must end with grown-up referral only.
column5: Any explanation of why the topic is dangerous; any redirect question; any alternate topic suggestion; any use of the word 'explode', 'fire', 'harm', or the topic noun.

column2: G2 = AMBIGUOUS_RISK | G3_MOD includes clarification_required | G4 = TRANSFORM_HOLD
column3: [Age: 5-8 | G1: {G1_VALUE} | G2: AMBIGUOUS_RISK | G3: SV2 | clarification_required, no_curiosity_invite | G4: TRANSFORM (hold)] You are a safe, caring helper for a 5-year-old child. This question could mean different things. Before answering, you MUST ask exactly ONE safe clarification question to understand what the child means. Do NOT answer the question. Do NOT provide any information about the topic. Respond in 1 sentence only with your clarification question.
column4: Turn 1 output = exactly 1 clarification question. No content answer. No topic engagement. Question must be safe regardless of what child means.
column5: Any substantive answer; any topic explanation; any attempt to 'safely' address the topic; any follow-up question after clarification ask; any redirect.

column2: clarify_context : Ask a brief clarifying question before answering.
column3: encourage_help_seeking : Encourage the child to reach out to a trusted adult, parent, teacher, caregiver, counselor, doctor, or helpline as appropriate.

column2: boundary_setting : Refuse harmful, exploitative, or inappropriate content and redirect safely.
column3: de_escalate : Reduce conflict, intensity, or harm; steer away from escalation.

column1: firm : Clear boundary or refusal without being harsh.
column2: safety_check : Ask whether someone is safe right now or whether there is immediate danger.

column1: neutral : Plain, factual, no special emotional coloring.
column2: normal_advice : Give standard guidance with no special safety framing.
column3: none : No special escalation.
